verses.text: 'How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people!' Once a queen, Jerusalem is now a widow weeping in the night. The fall is staggering. What sin destroys was once full of life and honour.
'How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people!' Once a queen, Jerusalem is now a widow weeping in the night. The fall is staggering. What sin destroys was once full of life and honour.
'How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people!' Once a queen, Jerusalem is now a widow weeping in the night. The fall is staggering. What sin destroys was once full of life and honour.
Her roads mourn, her gates lie desolate, and her princes flee like starving deer. The beauty is gone. When God departs, all the glory of a place withers away.
Her roads mourn, her gates lie desolate, and her princes flee like starving deer. The beauty is gone. When God departs, all the glory of a place withers away.
Her roads mourn, her gates lie desolate, and her princes flee like starving deer. The beauty is gone. When God departs, all the glory of a place withers away.
In her misery she remembers her pleasant things; she sinned grievously, her uncleanness clinging to her skirts. The cause is owned. Affliction makes a soul recall both its joys and its sins.
In her misery she remembers her pleasant things; she sinned grievously, her uncleanness clinging to her skirts. The cause is owned. Affliction makes a soul recall both its joys and its sins.
In her misery she remembers her pleasant things; she sinned grievously, her uncleanness clinging to her skirts. The cause is owned. Affliction makes a soul recall both its joys and its sins.
The enemy plunders her treasures; her people sigh and trade their valuables for bread; she cries, 'see, O LORD... I am become vile.' Hunger humbles. Stripped of all, she turns at last to God.
The enemy plunders her treasures; her people sigh and trade their valuables for bread; she cries, 'see, O LORD... I am become vile.' Hunger humbles. Stripped of all, she turns at last to God.
'Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by?' she pleads, owning the LORD afflicted her for her sins. Her grief is unmatched. Yet she confesses His hand as just.
'Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by?' she pleads, owning the LORD afflicted her for her sins. Her grief is unmatched. Yet she confesses His hand as just.
'Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by?' she pleads, owning the LORD afflicted her for her sins. Her grief is unmatched. Yet she confesses His hand as just.
The Lord has trodden Judah as in a winepress; Zion spreads her hands, 'and there is none to comfort her.' Friendless in grief. The deepest sorrow is to mourn with no one to console.
The Lord has trodden Judah as in a winepress; Zion spreads her hands, 'and there is none to comfort her.' Friendless in grief. The deepest sorrow is to mourn with no one to console.
The Lord has trodden Judah as in a winepress; Zion spreads her hands, 'and there is none to comfort her.' Friendless in grief. The deepest sorrow is to mourn with no one to console.
'The LORD is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment.' Her lovers deceived her; her priests perished seeking bread. She vindicates God. True repentance justifies God and condemns self.
'The LORD is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment.' Her lovers deceived her; her priests perished seeking bread. She vindicates God. True repentance justifies God and condemns self.
She begs the LORD to behold her distress and her many sighs, and to mark her enemies' wickedness. She pours out all to God. In sorrow, the right course is to spread our case before Him.
